summary: To analyze whether there’s an association between maternal iron supplement usage and infant birthweight, unpaired/independent samples T-test will be appropriate as it compares mean differences across two independent group categories—those who took iron supplements versus those who did not—and assumes normal distributions within each category based on available sample size considerations suitable for such analysis involving quantitative outcome measures like birthweights derived continuously over wide ranges typical among human populations studied clinically regarding nutritional interventions' effects prenatally. Agent1 recommends using Unpaired/Independent t-tests due to its suitability when comparing averages between non-related groups while assuming comparable variance conditions hold true per standard
